channel.title: The Brussels Times
channel.description: Latest Brussels posts from The Brussels Times.

item.title: Brussels emergency services remain disrupted as firefighters' strike continues
item.description: Firefighters have urged the public to be extra cautious and, where possible, avoid activities that carry a high risk of fire, accident or injury.

item.title: These are Belgium's most popular names – how common is yours?
item.description: Belgium's most common first names are declining, but you can now look up how popular your name.

item.title: 'I refuse to give up': Woman left heartbroken after cat disappears at Brussels Airport
item.description: “Mishu is not just a pet, he is a member of our family. We are devastated and desperately want to bring him home."

item.title: How Brussels became a French-speaking city
item.description: The Francisation of Brussels is known as the "oil slick effect" in Flanders, but how did French really take root in the Belgian capital?

item.title: Home from home: How does the EU bubble really feel about Belgium?
item.description: Belgium has gained a reputation for bad weather, average food and overly complex politics. But what do Brussels' international residents really think of the place they now call home?

item.title: From Schuman to Midi: How the Brussels 'master architect' wants a quality city
item.description: "Ugliness is not an argument. What do we need? How do we use the resources that are there?"

item.title: You know about the euro and the Belgian franc – but have you heard of the Brussels Zinne?
item.description: The Belgian franc is just one of a long list of currencies that have been used within the borders of present day Belgium.

item.title: National Ball: Place du Jeu de Balle fills up with a friendly atmosphere
item.description: The traditional National Ball held on the eve of Belgium’s National Day kicked off on Monday evening at Place du Jeu de Balle in Brussels.

item.title: Final whistle during World Cup triggered seismic tremors in Spain
item.description: Seismometers recorded ground vibrations after Torres' goal as well.

item.title: Brussels traffic to be disrupted on Belgian National Day
item.description: Several roads across the capital will close to make way for the Belgian National Day celebrations.

item.title: More than a dialect: Why Brusseleir remains the soul of Brussels
item.description: Brusseleir is much more than a mixture of French and Dutch. For those who still speak it, it embodies the city's humour, self-deprecation and famously surreal spirit

item.title: How Belgium learned to sell salad
item.description: As consumers embrace healthier, more sustainable ways of eating, a new generation of Belgian food brands have found the sweet spot.

item.title: Suspicious Brussels death linked to shooting
item.description: A person died after being found injured in a shooting shortly before 12.30am, with a ballistics expert and forensic team called in as a murder inquiry began.

item.title: Animal rights activists protest for more pigeon food in Brussels
item.description: Campaigners say pigeons are receiving little protection despite their numbers, and argue contraception and a clearer feeding policy could reduce disease, injury and suffering in the city.

item.title: 146th edition of Foire du Midi kicks off
item.description: This year’s event combines scale with change: 123 rides and stalls, a redesigned wheel with enclosed cabins, and a day set aside for 360 children facing illness, disability or hardship.

item.title: Electric scooter catches fire in Brussels
item.description: Firefighters said a smoke detector was installed in the flat where the blaze began, as they renewed warnings over charging device batteries and keeping exits clear.

item.title: F-35 and drones to take centre stage at Belgian National Day festivities
item.description: DJ sets, exhibitions, vehicle parades and more: here is the full schedule of Belgium's National Day celebrations.

item.title: Flemish government plans for 'sustainable future' of local airports
item.description: Mobility Minister Annick De Ridder stressed that the government is not planning to close any airports, focusing on the efficiency of their operations instead.

item.title: Roll up, roll up: The Foire du Midi is back – but Metro Line 3 is casting a shadow over the fun
item.description: "The fair has been split in two, and it simply isn't as beautiful," Patrick De Corte, president of the Brussels Showmen's Union, told The Brussels Times.

item.title: How Brussels wants to better integrate nightlife with a '24/7 vision'
item.description: "Nightlife and party culture are part of the very soul of Brussels."
